commands.review_summary:
The branch adds runnable structured Doctor health checks and converts plugin registry, configured plugin install, legacy plugin manifest, and related Doctor repair flows to return structured findings, changes, effects, diffs, and warnings.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a structured Doctor repair conversion PR, not a bug report with a failing current-main reproduction path. The relevant checks are source/contract review, focused tests, and current-head real behavior proof for the converted repair flows.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer-labeled stacked PR needs maintainer contract review and current-head proof rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: Doctor: continue structured repair conversion
target.body:
## Goal

Move plugin registry, configured plugin install, and legacy plugin manifest repairs onto the structured Doctor repair contract from #83753.

This wave uses the new basis deliberately:

- plugin registry repair is a direct `run(ctx)` check because the rule is naturally one flow: inspect registry state, build the repair plan, and either apply it when `ctx.repair` is true or return `status: "repairable"` when it is false
- configured plugin installs are also direct `run(ctx)` checks because release/install repair already follows the common boolean repair pattern
- legacy plugin manifest migration stays behind `defineSplitHealthCheck({ detect, repair })` because file detection and file rewrite are cleanly separable

The returned rule result carries the structured pieces the runner can present across surfaces: `findings`, `status`, `changes`, `effects`, `diffs`, `warnings`, `config`, and `reason`. Lint consumes findings, dry-run consumes the repairable plan, diff consumes file/config diffs, and fix applies config or side effects only through the repair branch.

Notes remain presentation-only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, and no runner note arrays.

## Converted Here

- `core/doctor/legacy-plugin-manifests` detects legacy top-level plugin manifest capability keys and rewrites them under `contracts` through the split adapter.
- `core/doctor/configured-plugin-installs` reports configured plugin/channel install repair as a direct `run(ctx)` plan/apply rule.
- `core/doctor/plugin-registry` reports stale registry, stale bundled install records, managed npm package shadows, and broken managed npm peer links as a direct `run(ctx)` plan/apply rule.
- The Doctor health contribution path routes these repairs through the structured runner while preserving legacy repair order.

## Invariants

This PR should stay isomorphic with the legacy plugin repair behavior:

- lint and repair start from the same plugin findings
- repair only acts on scoped findings when validation or targeted repair supplies them
- `ctx.repair: false` returns a non-mutating repair plan with `status: "repairable"`
- `ctx.repair: true` applies the existing repair paths and returns config/effects/warnings
- dry-run and diff are runner presentations over the returned plan, not separate rule logic
- plugin notes remain suppressible presentation, not machine-readable lint signal

## Review Surface

The main review question is whether these plugin repairs are now expressed in the right layer:

- direct `run(ctx)` for bool-shaped plugin registry/configured install rules
- split adapter for manifest migration where detect and rewrite are naturally separate
- structured effects for package/state/config/file side effects
- scoped validation after repair without reintroducing broad or duplicated discovery

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-84290-rebase.tsbuildinfo`
- `codex review --base fork/doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Plugin registry, configured plugin install, and legacy plugin manifest repairs now return structured Doctor repair plans and apply through the #83753 `run(ctx)` contract or its split adapter.

Real environment tested: WSL source checkout on branch `doctor-next-plugin-registry`, rebased on #83753 head `6de24ca856`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-84290-rebase.tsbuildinfo`
- `codex review --base fork/doctor-detection-interactive-maintenance`

Evidence after fix: 5 focused Doctor/plugin test files passed, 87 tests total. The core test tsconfig passed. Autoreview reported no discrete correctness issues against the stacked #83753 base.

Observed result after fix: Plugin registry and configured install checks now return findings plus repairable plans when non-mutating, and apply existing plugin repair paths only when `ctx.repair` is true. Legacy manifest repair remains split but normalizes through the same runner contract.

What was not tested: Full platform-wide Doctor scenarios and later repair-stack PRs were not rerun in this PR worktree; those should be handled as each subsequent stacked PR is rebuilt.
